title: Was the First Public Health Campaign Successful?

construct_vars: gvar_tbl <- df %>% dplyr::filter(city_reporting_required == 1) %>% dplyr::group_by(state_city_id) %>% dplyr::summarise(first_yr = min(year), .groups = 'drop')
df <- df %>% dplyr::left_join(gvar_tbl, by = 'state_city_id') %>% dplyr::mutate(gvar_CS = ifelse(!is.na(first_yr), as.integer(first_yr), 0L))

original_result.source: Table 3B, Column 1 (ln_tb_lungs, city_reporting_required only, full controls + city-specific trends). population-weighted. cluster(state_fips). N=7,439.

notes: Anti-TB public health campaign 1900-1917. 548 cities (91 treated, 457 never-treated). 16 staggered cohorts (1900-1917). Unbalanced panel (53% fill rate). city-specific linear trends via state_city_id[year] in fixest. Population weights (population_imp). Clustering at state level (42 states). Missing indicator method for covariates. Table 3B col 1 uses only city_reporting_required (not all 13 treatments). gvar_CS derived as first year city_reporting_required==1 per state_city_id. | AUDIT 2026-04-19: Paper-auditor EXACT (0.81%). SIGN REVERSAL: TWFE -0.036 vs CS-NT +0.020 / CS-NYT +0.012 — 16 cohorts 1900-1917 with extreme cohort-specific heterogeneity (Bacon TVT dominant, Treated-vs-Untreated range −0.354 to +0.285). 13th Lesson 7 direct-level Spec A collapse (7 RCS ctrls × staggered → 0/NA all variants). TWFE attenuation is cohort-group weighting drives CS reversal — TWFE uses all pairs including forbidden LvE/EvL with cross-cancellation; CS uses uniform group×time weighting revealing late-cohorts positive. Gardner/BJS consistent with TWFE (per skeptic). HonestDiD not robust at any Mbar = design fragility.
